messages.content: What type of native grassland dominated the eastern one-third of the U.S. before European settlement?
The type of native grassland that dominated the eastern one-third of the U.S. before European settlement was known as the Tallgrass Prairie.

Which one of the following forage grasses is the least cold-tolerant?


Smooth bromegrass


Timothy


Meadow bromegrass


Orchardgrass
The least cold-tolerant forage grass among the options provided is Timothy.

Which one of the following best describes the benefits of incorporating prairie strips in Iowa farmland as a conservation practice?


Which one of the following best describes the benefits of incorporating prairie strips in Iowa farmland as a conservation practice?


Prairie strips increase habitat for pollinators and other beneficial insects.


Prairie strips improve water quality by reducing nutrient runoff from agricultural fields


Prairie strips reduce the loss of soil from Iowa farmland


All of the above

All of the above benefits are associated with incorporating prairie strips in Iowa farmland as a conservation practice. Prairie strips can increase habitat for pollinators and other beneficial insects, improve water quality by reducing nutrient runoff from agricultural fields, and reduce soil loss from Iowa farmland.

In a “dead zone” of a lake or coastal water, what has been depleted to make it dead?


In a “dead zone” of a lake or coastal water, what has been depleted to make it dead?


Phosphorus


Carbon dioxide


Oxygen


Nitrogen

In a "dead zone" of a lake or coastal water, the substance that has been depleted to make it dead is oxygen. The excessive accumulation of nutrients, particularly nitrogen and phosphorus from sources like agricultural runoff or wastewater, can lead to excessive algal growth. When these algae die and decompose, oxygen in the water is consumed, resulting in oxygen depletion and the creation of a dead zone.

Which one of the following meristems carries out the elongation of a grass stem, resulting in the elevation of the seedhead during reproductive growth?


Which one of the following meristems carries out the elongation of a grass stem, resulting in the elevation of the seedhead during reproductive growth?


Apical meristem


Root meristem


Axillary bud meristem


Intercalary meristem
The apical meristem carries out the elongation of a grass stem, resulting in the elevation of the seedhead during reproductive growth.

Which type of grass inflorescence has the most branching and sub-branching in the arrangement of the spikelets?


Which type of grass inflorescence has the most branching and sub-branching in the arrangement of the spikelets?


Panicle


Spike


Raceme
The type of grass inflorescence that has the most branching and sub-branching in the arrangement of the spikelets is the panicle.

When U.S. corn silage production is mapped, it coincides with which one of the following types of animal production?


When U.S. corn silage production is mapped, it coincides with which one of the following types of animal production?


Beef


Dairy


Horse


Sheep

When U.S. corn silage production is mapped, it coincides primarily with dairy production. Corn silage is often used as a feed source for dairy cattle due to its high energy and digestibility content. It is less commonly utilized in beef, horse, or sheep production.

Kernza is a perennial wheat that was selected from intermediate wheatgrass. Which one of the characteristics below does not belong to kernza?


Kernza is a perennial wheat that was selected from intermediate wheatgrass. Which one of the characteristics below does not belong to kernza?


As a perennial, kernza develops a much more extensive root system than annual wheat.


Successful selection of kernza seed size and seedhead number means that kernza yields nearly equally with annual wheat.


Kernza not only provides grain but can be grazed following the annual grain harvest, also providing considerable forage dry matter.

The characteristic that does not belong to kernza is: "Successful selection of kernza seed size and seedhead number means that kernza yields nearly equally with annual wheat."

While kernza is a perennial wheat that develops a more extensive root system than annual wheat and can be grazed following the grain harvest, one of the challenges with kernza is its lower grain yield compared to annual wheat. Therefore, it does not yield nearly equally with annual wheat.

What proportion of the fresh ground water in the U.S. is located in the Ogallala Aquifer?


What proportion of the fresh ground water in the U.S. is located in the Ogallala Aquifer?


30%


10%


40%


20%
Approximately 30% of the fresh groundwater in the United States is located in the Ogallala Aquifer.

What are the weather conditions during which photorespiration is most likely to occur?


What are the weather conditions during which photorespiration is most likely to occur?


Warm, sunny weather in the spring


Extended drought in late summer


Cool, overcast periods in the fall

Photorespiration is most likely to occur during cool, overcast periods in the fall. During these conditions, when light intensity is lower and temperatures are cooler, the ratio of oxygen to carbon dioxide in plant tissues can become imbalanced, leading to increased photorespiration.

Grasses like big bluestem have “C4” photosynthesis in which the capture of carbon dioxide (CO2) from the air is done by some cells, while the use of CO2 to make sugars takes place in other cells. Which cells in the leaves of C4 plants are specialized to capture CO2?


Grasses like big bluestem have “C4” photosynthesis in which the capture of carbon dioxide (CO2) from the air is done by some cells, while the use of CO2 to make sugars takes place in other cells. Which cells in the leaves of C4 plants are specialized to capture CO2?


The fiber cells


The xylem cells


The bundle sheath cells


The mesophyll cells

In C4 plants like big bluestem grass, the specialized cells in the leaves that are responsible for capturing carbon dioxide (CO2) from the air are the bundle sheath cells. These cells are located around the vascular tissue and are involved in the initial fixation of CO2 as a four-carbon compound. The CO2 is then passed on to the neighboring mesophyll cells for sugar synthesis through the Calvin cycle.

Aerobic respiration that takes place in mitochondria uses sugars and oxygen (O2) and results in the loss of CO2 from plant cells. What is the ultimate end product of aerobic respiration?


Aerobic respiration that takes place in mitochondria uses sugars and oxygen (O2) and results in the loss of CO2 from plant cells. What is the ultimate end product of aerobic respiration?


ATP


Pyruvate


FADH2

The ultimate end product of aerobic respiration in plant cells is ATP (adenosine triphosphate). ATP is the primary form of energy used by cells for various metabolic processes. While pyruvate and FADH2 are intermediates in aerobic respiration, they are not the final end products.
